content: The human brain’s active memory mechanism, which relies on long-distance neural communication to sustain plasticity for ongoing learning, can be redesigned as a biological intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) for next-generation 2025 autonomous agents. This framework replaces the standard ICM’s focus on only rewarding novel state discovery with an active memory-plasticity loop that reinforces curiosity-driven exploration by integrating new findings into existing long-term agent memory, mirroring how the human brain sustains adaptive open-ended learning.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
